title: KPI Bar (Top of Dashboard)
location: Always visible at top of main page
whatYouSee: Four metric cards: Cash on Hand ($12.68M), Runway (~10.6mo), AR Overdue ($186K), AP at Risk ($78K). Color-coded health badges (Critical, High Risk, etc.).
whatItMeans: Your 10-second financial vital signs. Cash and runway tell you survival timeline. AR overdue is money stuck with customers. AP at risk is money you might pay incorrectly.
whatToDo: If Runway shows Critical — open Cash Forecast and CFO Briefing immediately. If AR Overdue is High Risk — prioritize Collections tab. If AP at Risk is elevated — review Payables before approving payments.

title: CFO Briefing
whatYouSee: Financial Snapshot Card (4 vitals + 9-month projection), computed verdict headline, Executive Pulse with RED/YELLOW items, 'Agents Handled Automatically' chips. ↻ Update button top right.
whatItMeans: The synthesized view across all 7 agents. The verdict headline is computed from live data — e.g. 'Cash hits $3M warning in 7 months — burn 34% above target'.
whatToDo: Click ↻ Update first (~60 sec). Read the verdict headline on the Financial Snapshot. Work through RED items in the Action Items sidebar on the right. Click any agent chip to drill down.

title: Treasury
whatYouSee: 8-week cash balance area chart (operating + reserve), weekly burn bar chart, AR/AP waterfall cards, agent conclusion cards (RED/YELLOW/GREEN), collapsed audit trail.
whatItMeans: Day-to-day cash health. Shows whether burn is accelerating and how cash balance is trending week over week. In Hypo mode, shows a green dashed sim overlay.
whatToDo: Compare weekly burn bars to the $196K–$231K guidance line mentally. Investigate any RED conclusion about burn overage. In Hypo mode, check if sim approvals adjust the cash line.

title: Cash Forecast
whatYouSee: 9-month line chart with three scenario lines (current trajectory, spending discipline, revenue growth). Agent conclusions about when cash becomes critical.
whatItMeans: Forward-looking view. Answers 'when do we run out of money if nothing changes?' and 'how much do cuts or revenue help?'
whatToDo: Find the month where the current trajectory line crosses your minimum cash threshold. Compare discipline vs growth scenarios to frame board options.

title: Budget
whatYouSee: Grouped bar chart (planned vs actual by department), variance table with dollar and % over/under, agent conclusions on overspend departments.
whatItMeans: Department-level accountability. Shows *who* is driving burn, not just the total. 8 departments × 6 months of data.
whatToDo: Sort variance table for biggest overruns. Conference/marketing overspend (MODEX $45K, ProMat $32K) often appears here. Discuss with department owners.

title: Collections (AR)
whatYouSee: AR aging bar chart by bucket, invoice table (customer, amount, days overdue, last contact), agent conclusions. MidWest Fulfillment $48K at 92 days is a key example.
whatItMeans: Money customers owe you. Aging shows collection risk. High days overdue = cash trapped and potential bad debt.
whatToDo: Prioritize 90+ day invoices. Call MidWest Fulfillment immediately. Track DSO trend. Use Send Reminder for 30–60 day accounts.

title: Payables (AP & Vendors)
whatYouSee: AP invoice table sorted by overdue days, fraud audit section, payment hold flags, agent conclusions. Apex Logistics duplicate invoices ($14.5K) is a key example.
whatItMeans: Money you owe vendors. Fraud audit catches duplicates and suspicious charges before cash leaves the building.
whatToDo: Never pay invoices flagged as duplicates. Resolve payment holds within 48 hours. Schedule Pay only after fraud audit clears.

title: Contracts
whatYouSee: Vendor table sorted by risk/exposure: contract type, monthly spend, department owner, renewal notes. Flags M2M and missing contracts.
whatItMeans: Vendor relationship risk. High spend without contract protection = pricing and continuity risk.
whatToDo: Renegotiate top 5 unprotected high-spend vendors. Convert M2M to annual where possible. Calendar renewals 90 days out.

title: Transactions
whatYouSee: Filterable table of 69 transactions: date, vendor, amount, category, department, status. CSV export.
whatItMeans: Granular drill-down beneath the summary views. Every dollar traced to a vendor and department.
whatToDo: Use when an agent flags something and you need proof. Filter by vendor (e.g. Apex Logistics) or department to investigate spikes.

title: Action Items
location: Right sidebar (persistent) or Top nav → Data ▾ → All Actions
whatYouSee: Right sidebar: open items with Approve/Delegate buttons, delegated items, resolved items. In Hypo mode: Simulation Ledger strip showing cumulative impact. Full panel via Data ▾.
whatItMeans: The decision queue. RED agent conclusions auto-create action items. Approve/Delegate triggers an AI-generated consequence brief and draft email.
whatToDo: In Live mode: approve or delegate each open item after reading the AI brief. In Hypo mode: sim-approve to preview impact, then switch to Live to commit for real.

title: Agent Status Bar
whatYouSee: Provider pill (Cerebras/Groq/SambaNova), model name, status dot, last run time, Re-run button. Below: database table provenance labels (e.g. 'ar_aging · 15 invoices').
whatItMeans: Shows which AI model analyzed this tab and when, and exactly which data it read. Provider colors: purple=Cerebras, orange=Groq, emerald=SambaNova.
whatToDo: If status is error, re-run the agent. Check data provenance labels to confirm the agent read current data. Use ↻ Update to refresh all agents.

title: Conclusion Cards
location: Below charts on every agent tab
whatYouSee: RED, YELLOW, or GREEN cards with title and body text. Extracted from agent output by the parser.
whatItMeans: The agent's prioritized findings. RED = act now. YELLOW = watch. GREEN = healthy.
whatToDo: Read RED cards first. Cross-reference with charts above. Expand Audit Trail if you doubt a number.

title: Audit Trail
location: Collapsed section below conclusion cards on agent tabs
whatYouSee: Three collapsible sections: Data Scanned (sources), Reasoning Chain (logic steps), Full Report (markdown narrative).
whatItMeans: Transparency layer. Proves the agent read real data and shows its reasoning — essential for trusting AI in finance.
whatToDo: Expand when a conclusion surprises you. Verify the data sources match what you expect. Challenge reasoning before acting.
